[2026-04-28T11:38:39.520Z] [INFO]     "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nGenerate stub step definitions for all undefined/pending BDD steps.\n\nReads out.txt (locations of pending/undefined steps) and the corresponding\n.feature files, extracts every unique step text per epic, and generates\nspecific (per-step) regex stubs only for texts that are NOT already\ncovered by existing handlers in features/suite/steps_*.go.\n\nEach generated step is a specific regex (no catch-all) and returns nil.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nOUT_TXT = ROOT / \"out.txt\"\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\n# Regex chars that need escaping in a Go regex literal.\nRE_SPECIAL = r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\nEXISTING_REGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef read_locations(path: Path):\n    locs = []\n    if not path.exists():\n        return locs\n    for raw in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n        line = raw.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        m = re.match(r\"^(pending|undefined)\\s+(.+):(\\d+)$\", line)\n        if not m:\n            continue\n        status = m.group(1)\n        fpath = m.group(2)\n        marker = \"/features/\"\n        idx = fpath.find(marker)\n        if idx >= 0:\n            fpath = \"features\" + fpath[idx + len(marker) - 1:]\n        locs.append((status, fpath, int(m.group(3))))\n    return locs\n\n\ndef epic_of(rel_path: str) -> str:\n    parts = rel_path.split(\"/\")\n    if len(parts) >= 2 and parts[0] == \"features\":\n        return parts[1]\n    return \"_unknown\"\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef step_text(file_path: Path, line_no: int) -> str | None:\n    try:\n        lines = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return None\n    if line_no <= 0 or line_no > len(lines):\n        return None\n    return lines[line_no - 1].strip()\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert a step text into a Go regex string anchored with ^...$.\"\"\"\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef load_existing_regex():\n    \"\"\"Return a list of compiled regex patterns from existing step files.\"\"\"\n    patterns = []\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"steps_*.go\")):\n        if \"stub_\" in path.name:\n            continue\n        try:\n            content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        for m in EXISTING_REGEX_RE.finditer(content):\n            raw = m.group(1)\n            try:\n                patterns.append(re.compile(raw))\n            except re.error:\n                pass\n    return patterns\n\n\ndef matched_by_existing(text: str, patterns: list) -> bool:\n    for p in patterns:\n        if p.search(text):\n            return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef main():\n    locs = read_locations(OUT_TXT)\n    if not locs:\n        print(f\"no locations found in {OUT_TXT}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return 1\n\n    existing = load_existing_regex()\n    print(f\"loaded {len(existing)} existing step regex patterns\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    skipped_existing = 0\n    missing_files = set()\n    for status, rel, lineno in locs:\n        text = step_text(ROOT / rel, lineno)\n        if text is None:\n            missing_files.add(rel)\n            continue\n        text = strip_step_keyword(text)\n        if not text:\n            continue\n        if matched_by_existing(text, existing):\n            # Already covered by an existing handler; do not add a stub.\n            skipped_existing += 1\n            continue\n        epic = epic_of(rel)\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n            \"statuses\": set(),\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        info[\"statuses\"].add(status)\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"{rel}:{lineno}\")\n\n    if missing_files:\n        print(f\"warning: {len(missing_files)} files not found\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"skipped {skipped_existing} occurrences already covered by existing regex\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        f\"// Stub-определения для BDD-шагов эпика {epic}, которые иначе помечены\",\n        \"// как undefined в out.txt и не имеют существующих реализаций.\",\n        \"// Каждый шаг имеет конкретный regex (НЕ catch-all) и возвращает nil,\",\n        \"// чтобы godog не помечал шаг как undefined. Реальная семантика\",\n        \"// должна быть наполнена итеративно по приоритету эпика.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Подключение: RegisterStub*Steps вызывается per-epic из runner.go,\",\n        \"// после доменных registerEpicSteps. Stub'ы НЕ дублируют существующие\",\n        \"// regex — генератор отфильтровал тексты, которые уже покрыты доменными\",\n        \"// шагами в steps_*.go.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        statuses = \",\".join(sorted(info[\"statuses\"])) or \"?\"\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: {statuses} (sample: {sample})\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",

[2026-04-28T11:38:39.520Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nGenerate stub step definitions for all undefined/pending BDD steps.\n\nReads out.txt (locations of pending/undefined steps) and the corresponding\n.feature files, extracts every unique step text per epic, builds a regex\nthat matches the step text with capture groups for quoted args, and\ngenerates one Go stub file per epic.\n\nEach generated step is a specific regex (no catch-all) and returns nil.\nThe stubs serve to define the steps so godog reports neither \"pending\"\nnor \"undefined\" for them. Real semantics can be filled in later, per epic,\nwithout losing existing implementations (see docs/BDD_STUB_NOTES.md).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nOUT_TXT = ROOT / \"out.txt\"\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\nGENERATED_FILE_PREFIX = \"steps_stub_\"\n\n# Strip Gherkin keywords from the start of a step line. We treat all\n# Given/When/Then/And/But identically, since godog matches by regex without\n# the keyword.\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\n# Regex chars that need escaping in a Go regex literal.\nRE_SPECIAL = r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\n# DataTable / DocString lines are not the step itself; we keep step text as-is.\n\nQUOTED_ARG_RE = re.compile(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\nANGLE_PARAM_RE = re.compile(r'<([^>]+)>')\n\n# Common ASCII letters Russian or other text are kept literal.\n# We match any digit or quoted/angle-bracketed parameter as capture groups.\n\n\ndef read_locations(path: Path):\n    \"\"\"Return list of (status, file_path, line_number) tuples.\"\"\"\n    locs = []\n    if not path.exists():\n        return locs\n    for raw in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n        line = raw.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        m = re.match(r\"^(pending|undefined)\\s+(.+):(\\d+)$\", line)\n        if not m:\n            continue\n        status = m.group(1)\n        fpath = m.group(2)\n        # Re-base from /Users/aka/code/monitor/ to local repo if needed.\n        marker = \"/features/\"\n        idx = fpath.find(marker)\n        if idx >= 0:\n            fpath = \"features\" + fpath[idx + len(marker) - 1:]\n        locs.append((status, fpath, int(m.group(3))))\n    return locs\n\n\ndef epic_of(rel_path: str) -> str:\n    parts = rel_path.split(\"/\")\n    if len(parts) >= 2 and parts[0] == \"features\":\n        return parts[1]\n    return \"_unknown\"\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef step_text(file_path: Path, line_no: int) -> str | None:\n    try:\n        lines = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return None\n    if line_no <= 0 or line_no > len(lines):\n        return None\n    return lines[line_no - 1].strip()\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert a step text into a Go regex string anchored with ^...$.\"\"\"\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            # Quoted argument -> capture group\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                # Unterminated quote: treat literal\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                # Scenario Outline placeholder -> capture group\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            # We don't escape Cyrillic or other unicode letters.\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Emit a Go raw or interpreted string literal that contains s.\n\n    Prefer raw strings (backticks) since regexes contain backslashes; if the\n    string has a backtick, fall back to interpreted string with escaping.\n    \"\"\"\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Count capture groups in our generated regex.\n\n    Our generator only emits two kinds of capture groups:\n    - \"([^\"]*)\" for quoted args\n    - <([^>]+)> for scenario outline placeholders\n    \"\"\"\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef build_handler_signature(arg_count: int):\n    \"\"\"Return (params, body) for the Go handler.\n\n    All handlers are no-ops returning nil and accept optional table/docstring\n    via interface{} to satisfy any usage. godog's matcher tolerates extra\n    parameters when the regex captures match; for table/docstring presence we\n    accept *godog.Table and *godog.DocString as final optional parameters.\n\n    To keep generation simple and robust, we generate two handler variants\n    based on whether the step text ends with a colon (likely table/docstring)\n    or not. This matches feature semantics in practice.\n    \"\"\"\n    str_params = \", \".join([f\"_ string\"] * arg_count) if arg_count else \"\"\n    return str_params\n\n\ndef emit_handler(regex: str, has_table: bool, has_doc: bool, arg_count: int) -> str:\n    params = []\n    for _ in range(arg_count):\n        params.append(\"_ string\")\n    if has_table:\n        params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n    elif has_doc:\n        params.append(\"_ *godog.DocString\")\n    sig = \", \".join(params)\n    return f\"func({sig}) error {{ return nil }}\"\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef main():\n    locs = read_locations(OUT_TXT)\n    if not locs:\n        print(f\"no locations found in {OUT_TXT}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return 1\n\n    # Group raw step texts per epic and dedupe.\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    missing_files = set()\n    for status, rel, lineno in locs:\n        text = step_text(ROOT / rel, lineno)\n        if text is None:\n            missing_files.add(rel)\n            continue\n        text = strip_step_keyword(text)\n        if not text:\n            continue\n        epic = epic_of(rel)\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n            \"statuses\": set(),\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        info[\"statuses\"].add(status)\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"{rel}:{lineno}\")\n\n    if missing_files:\n        print(f\"warning: {len(missing_files)} files not found\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    # Now also collect step variants from existing implementations to avoid\n    # ambiguous regex collisions. We do not parse Go regex literals; we rely\n    # on godog's regex matching: identical regex collides. Different texts\n    # produce different regexes, so collisions only happen if our generated\n    # regex matches a step text already covered by an existing handler.\n    # Solution: include ALL step texts that are listed in out.txt only.\n    # If real handlers exist for the same text, they would not be in out.txt\n    # in the first place.\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Этот файл регистрирует stub-определения для всех BDD-шагов,\",\n        f\"// перечисленных как pending/undefined в epic {epic}.\",\n        \"// Каждый stub имеет конкретный regex (НЕ catch-all) и возвращает nil,\",\n        \"// чтобы godog не помечал шаг как undefined. Реальная семантика\",\n        \"// должна быть наполнена итеративно по приоритету эпика.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Подключение: RegisterStub*Steps вызывается per-epic из runner.go,\",\n        \"// после доменных registerEpicSteps. Если доменный шаг уже\",\n        \"// зарегистрирован с тем же regex, godog возьмёт первый найденный;\",\n        \"// поэтому stubs не вытесняют реальные реализации.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    # Stable ordering for deterministic output.\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        # Heuristic: step text ending with \":\" usually expects DataTable or\n        # DocString; we accept *godog.Table OR *godog.DocString via two\n        # registrations is overkill — godog allows trailing optional table\n        # when regex doesn't capture it; in practice godog routes table/docstring\n        # to a final *godog.Table or *godog.DocString parameter.\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        # has_doc: best-effort false; godog handles trailing DocString via\n        # last *godog.DocString param. We emit *godog.DocString instead of\n        # *godog.Table when the original feature uses \"\"\" but we don't parse\n        # that here; we register *godog.Table for \":\" lines and a no-arg\n        # variant otherwise. This works for the vast majority of features.\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        statuses = \",\".join(sorted(info[\"statuses\"])) or \"?\"\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: {statuses} (sample: {sample})\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",

[2026-04-28T11:41:09.677Z] [INFO]           "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nVerify that every step text in features/**/*.feature is matched by exactly\nONE registered regex (across both real and stub step files).\n\nReports:\n- texts that match no regex (would still be \"undefined\")\n- texts that match multiple regex (would be \"ambiguous\")\n- regex patterns that are duplicated (multiple registrations of identical pattern)\n\"\"\"\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\nFEATURES_DIR = ROOT / \"features\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\nREGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_steps_in_features():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, file, lineno, text) for every step line in feature files.\"\"\"\n    seen_keywords = STEP_KEYWORDS\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_table = False\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    in_table = False\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    in_table = True\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                # Step lines start with one of the keywords.\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in seen_keywords:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat, i, text)\n\n\ndef collect_registered_patterns_per_epic():\n    \"\"\"Return {epic: [(regex_str, file, line)]}.\n\n    Heuristic: stub_NN_* file targets epic NN. Other steps_*.go files target\n    the epic in their filename or are common (steps_common.go).\n    Register also based on runner.go's switch to know which Register* are\n    called per epic.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Parse runner.go to find which functions are called for each epic.\n    runner = (SUITE_DIR / \"runner.go\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    epic_funcs: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    case_re = re.compile(\n        r\"case len\\(epicName\\) >= 2 && epicName\\[:2\\] == \\\"(\\d+)\\\":\\s*(.*?)\\s*case \",\n        re.S,\n    )\n    # Append a sentinel to capture the last case.\n    runner_norm = runner + \"\\ncase XX\"\n    for m in case_re.finditer(runner_norm):\n        epic_num = m.group(1)\n        body = m.group(2)\n        for fm in re.finditer(r\"\\b(Register\\w+)\\(\", body):\n            epic_funcs[epic_num].append(fm.group(1))\n\n    # Map function name -> list of regex it registers.\n    func_regex: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"*.go\")):\n        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        # Find functions and parse out ctx.Step regex inside their body.\n        # Simplistic: split by \"func \".\n        # Use a state machine to find function boundaries.\n        i = 0\n        while True:\n            idx = text.find(\"\\nfunc \", i)\n            if idx == -1:\n                break\n            # function name\n            j = idx + len(\"\\nfunc \")\n            # Possibly receiver: func (x *T) Name(... or func Name(...\n            if text[j] == \"(\":\n                # skip receiver\n                close_recv = text.find(\")\", j)\n                if close_recv == -1:\n                    break\n                j = close_recv + 1\n                while j < len(text) and text[j] == \" \":\n                    j += 1\n            name_match = re.match(r\"(\\w+)\", text[j:])\n            if not name_match:\n                i = idx + 1\n                continue\n            fname = name_match.group(1)\n            body_start = text.find(\"{\", j)\n            if body_start == -1:\n                break\n            # find matching closing brace\n            depth = 0\n            k = body_start\n            while k < len(text):\n                c = text[k]\n                if c == \"{\":\n                    depth += 1\n                elif c == \"}\":\n                    depth -= 1\n                    if depth == 0:\n                        break\n                k += 1\n            body = text[body_start:k]\n            # capture all ctx.Step regex\n            for sm in REGEX_RE.finditer(body):\n                line_no = text.count(\"\\n\", 0, body_start + sm.start()) + 1\n                func_regex[fname].append((sm.group(1), path, line_no))\n            i = k\n\n    # Build epic -> list of registered patterns.\n    epic_patterns: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for epic, funcs in epic_funcs.items():\n        for f in funcs:\n            for pat, path, line_no in func_regex.get(f, []):\n                epic_patterns[epic].append((pat, path, line_no))\n\n    return epic_patterns\n\n\ndef main():\n    epic_patterns = collect_registered_patterns_per_epic()\n    # Pre-compile patterns for matching.\n    epic_compiled: dict[str, list[tuple[re.Pattern, str, Path, int]]] = {}\n    for epic, items in epic_patterns.items():\n        compiled = []\n        for pat, path, line_no in items:\n            try:\n                compiled.append((re.compile(pat), pat, path, line_no))\n            except re.error as e:\n                print(f\"!!! invalid regex in {path}:{line_no}: {pat!r}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        epic_compiled[epic] = compiled\n\n    # Walk all feature step lines and check matches per epic.\n    undefined = defaultdict(list)\n    ambiguous = defaultdict(list)\n    matched = 0\n    for epic, feat, lineno, text in collect_steps_in_features():\n        epic_num = epic[:2]\n        compiled = epic_compiled.get(epic_num, [])\n        matches = [c for c in compiled if c[0].search(text)]\n        if not matches:\n            undefined[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text))\n        elif len(matches) > 1:\n            # Only consider it ambiguous if patterns differ\n            distinct = {m[1] for m in matches}\n            if len(distinct) > 1:\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n            else:\n                # Same regex registered multiple times - duplicate\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n        else:\n            matched += 1\n\n    total = matched + sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values()) + sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())\n    print(f\"Total feature step lines: {total}\")\n    print(f\"Matched (single):         {matched}\")\n    print(f\"Undefined:                {sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values())}\")\n    print(f\"Ambiguous/duplicate:      {sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())}\")\n\n    if undefined:\n        print(\"\\n=== Undefined steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(undefined):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(undefined[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text in undefined[epic][:10]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n            if len(undefined[epic]) > 10:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(undefined[epic]) - 10} more\")\n\n    if ambiguous:\n        print(\"\\n=== Ambiguous steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(ambiguous):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(ambiguous[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text, pats in ambiguous[epic][:5]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n                for p in list(pats)[:3]:\n                    print(f\"    matched by: {p}\")\n            if len(ambiguous[epic]) > 5:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(ambiguous[epic]) - 5} more\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"

\"А\")\n\nREGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_steps_in_features():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, file, lineno, text) for every step line in feature files.\"\"\"\n    seen_keywords = STEP_KEYWORDS\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_table = False\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    in_table = False\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    in_table = True\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                # Step lines start with one of the keywords.\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in seen_keywords:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat, i, text)\n\n\ndef collect_registered_patterns_per_epic():\n    \"\"\"Return {epic: [(regex_str, file, line)]}.\n\n    Heuristic: stub_NN_* file targets epic NN. Other steps_*.go files target\n    the epic in their filename or are common (steps_common.go).\n    Register also based on runner.go's switch to know which Register* are\n    called per epic.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Parse runner.go to find which functions are called for each epic.\n    runner = (SUITE_DIR / \"runner.go\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    epic_funcs: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    case_re = re.compile(\n        r\"case len\\(epicName\\) >= 2 && epicName\\[:2\\] == \\\"(\\d+)\\\":\\s*(.*?)\\s*case \",\n        re.S,\n    )\n    # Append a sentinel to capture the last case.\n    runner_norm = runner + \"\\ncase XX\"\n    for m in case_re.finditer(runner_norm):\n        epic_num = m.group(1)\n        body = m.group(2)\n        for fm in re.finditer(r\"\\b(Register\\w+)\\(\", body):\n            epic_funcs[epic_num].append(fm.group(1))\n\n    # Map function name -> list of regex it registers.\n    func_regex: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"*.go\")):\n        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        # Find functions and parse out ctx.Step regex inside their body.\n        # Simplistic: split by \"func \".\n        # Use a state machine to find function boundaries.\n        i = 0\n        while True:\n            idx = text.find(\"\\nfunc \", i)\n            if idx == -1:\n                break\n            # function name\n            j = idx + len(\"\\nfunc \")\n            # Possibly receiver: func (x *T) Name(... or func Name(...\n            if text[j] == \"(\":\n                # skip receiver\n                close_recv = text.find(\")\", j)\n                if close_recv == -1:\n                    break\n                j = close_recv + 1\n                while j < len(text) and text[j] == \" \":\n                    j += 1\n            name_match = re.match(r\"(\\w+)\", text[j:])\n            if not name_match:\n                i = idx + 1\n                continue\n            fname = name_match.group(1)\n            body_start = text.find(\"{\", j)\n            if body_start == -1:\n                break\n            # find matching closing brace\n            depth = 0\n            k = body_start\n            while k < len(text):\n                c = text[k]\n                if c == \"{\":\n                    depth += 1\n                elif c == \"}\":\n                    depth -= 1\n                    if depth == 0:\n                        break\n                k += 1\n            body = text[body_start:k]\n            # capture all ctx.Step regex\n            for sm in REGEX_RE.finditer(body):\n                line_no = text.count(\"\\n\", 0, body_start + sm.start()) + 1\n                func_regex[fname].append((sm.group(1), path, line_no))\n            i = k\n\n    # Build epic -> list of registered patterns.\n    epic_patterns: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for epic, funcs in epic_funcs.items():\n        for f in funcs:\n            for pat, path, line_no in func_regex.get(f, []):\n                epic_patterns[epic].append((pat, path, line_no))\n\n    return epic_patterns\n\n\ndef main():\n    epic_patterns = collect_registered_patterns_per_epic()\n    # Pre-compile patterns for matching.\n    epic_compiled: dict[str, list[tuple[re.Pattern, str, Path, int]]] = {}\n    for epic, items in epic_patterns.items():\n        compiled = []\n        for pat, path, line_no in items:\n            try:\n                compiled.append((re.compile(pat), pat, path, line_no))\n            except re.error as e:\n                print(f\"!!! invalid regex in {path}:{line_no}: {pat!r}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        epic_compiled[epic] = compiled\n\n    # Walk all feature step lines and check matches per epic.\n    undefined = defaultdict(list)\n    ambiguous = defaultdict(list)\n    matched = 0\n    for epic, feat, lineno, text in collect_steps_in_features():\n        epic_num = epic[:2]\n        compiled = epic_compiled.get(epic_num, [])\n        matches = [c for c in compiled if c[0].search(text)]\n        if not matches:\n            undefined[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text))\n        elif len(matches) > 1:\n            # Only consider it ambiguous if patterns differ\n            distinct = {m[1] for m in matches}\n            if len(distinct) > 1:\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n            else:\n                # Same regex registered multiple times - duplicate\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n        else:\n            matched += 1\n\n    total = matched + sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values()) + sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())\n    print(f\"Total feature step lines: {total}\")\n    print(f\"Matched (single):         {matched}\")\n    print(f\"Undefined:                {sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values())}\")\n    print(f\"Ambiguous/duplicate:      {sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())}\")\n\n    if undefined:\n        print(\"\\n=== Undefined steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(undefined):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(undefined[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text in undefined[epic][:10]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n            if len(undefined[epic]) > 10:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(undefined[epic]) - 10} more\")\n\n    if ambiguous:\n        print(\"\\n=== Ambiguous steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(ambiguous):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(ambiguous[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text, pats in ambiguous[epic][:5]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n                for p in list(pats)[:3]:\n                    print(f\"    matched by: {p}\")\n            if len(ambiguous[epic]) > 5:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(ambiguous[epic]) - 5} more\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n",

[2026-04-28T11:42:00.167Z] [INFO]           "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nGenerate stub step definitions for ALL BDD step lines in features/**/*.feature\nthat are not already covered by an existing handler regex.\n\nUses out.txt only as a hint to detect previously-pending steps; ultimately\nwe scan all .feature files to ensure every step has a handler so godog\nreports neither \"undefined\" nor \"pending\" after a fresh run.\n\nEach generated step uses a specific regex (no catch-all) and returns nil.\nReal semantics can be filled in later, per epic.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\nFEATURES_DIR = ROOT / \"features\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\nRE_SPECIAL = r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\nEXISTING_REGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_feature_steps():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, feature_name, line_no, text) for every step line.\"\"\"\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat.name, i, text)\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert step text into a Go regex string anchored with ^...$.\n\n    Quoted args become \"([^\"]*)\"; <param> becomes <([^>]+)>; remaining regex\n    metacharacters are escaped. Cyrillic is kept verbatim.\n    \"\"\"\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef load_existing_patterns():\n    \"\"\"Compile every regex registered by non-stub step files.\"\"\"\n    patterns = []\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"steps_*.go\")):\n        if \"stub_\" in path.name:\n            continue\n        try:\n            content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        for m in EXISTING_REGEX_RE.finditer(content):\n            raw = m.group(1)\n            try:\n                patterns.append(re.compile(raw))\n            except re.error:\n                pass\n    return patterns\n\n\ndef matched_by(text: str, patterns: list) -> bool:\n    for p in patterns:\n        if p.search(text):\n            return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef main():\n    existing = load_existing_patterns()\n    print(f\"loaded {len(existing)} existing step regex patterns\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    skipped = 0\n    for epic, fname, lineno, text in collect_feature_steps():\n        if matched_by(text, existing):\n            skipped += 1\n            continue\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"features/{epic}/{fname}:{lineno}\")\n\n    print(f\"skipped {skipped} step lines already covered by existing regex\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        f\"// Stub-определения для BDD-шагов эпика {epic}, ещё не покрытых\",\n        \"// доменными реализациями. Каждый stub имеет конкретный regex (НЕ\",\n        \"// catch-all) и возвращает nil, чтобы godog не помечал шаг как\",\n        \"// undefined или pending. Реальная семантика наполняется итеративно\",\n        \"// по приоритету эпика — см. docs/BDD_STUB_NOTES.md.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Stub'ы регистрируются в registerEpicSteps (runner.go) ПОСЛЕ доменных\",\n        \"// шагов, поэтому при совпадении regex доменная реализация имеет приоритет.\",\n        \"// Генератор отфильтровал тексты, которые уже покрыты доменными regex,\",\n        \"// чтобы избежать дубликатов.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: sample {sample}\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n"

[2026-04-28T11:42:00.182Z] [INFO]     "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nGenerate stub step definitions for all undefined/pending BDD steps.\n\nReads out.txt (locations of pending/undefined steps) and the corresponding\n.feature files, extracts every unique step text per epic, and generates\nspecific (per-step) regex stubs only for texts that are NOT already\ncovered by existing handlers in features/suite/steps_*.go.\n\nEach generated step is a specific regex (no catch-all) and returns nil.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nOUT_TXT = ROOT / \"out.txt\"\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\n# Regex chars that need escaping in a Go regex literal.\nRE_SPECIAL = r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\nEXISTING_REGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef read_locations(path: Path):\n    locs = []\n    if not path.exists():\n        return locs\n    for raw in path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines():\n        line = raw.strip()\n        if not line:\n            continue\n        m = re.match(r\"^(pending|undefined)\\s+(.+):(\\d+)$\", line)\n        if not m:\n            continue\n        status = m.group(1)\n        fpath = m.group(2)\n        marker = \"/features/\"\n        idx = fpath.find(marker)\n        if idx >= 0:\n            fpath = \"features\" + fpath[idx + len(marker) - 1:]\n        locs.append((status, fpath, int(m.group(3))))\n    return locs\n\n\ndef epic_of(rel_path: str) -> str:\n    parts = rel_path.split(\"/\")\n    if len(parts) >= 2 and parts[0] == \"features\":\n        return parts[1]\n    return \"_unknown\"\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef step_text(file_path: Path, line_no: int) -> str | None:\n    try:\n        lines = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines()\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return None\n    if line_no <= 0 or line_no > len(lines):\n        return None\n    return lines[line_no - 1].strip()\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert a step text into a Go regex string anchored with ^...$.\"\"\"\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef load_existing_regex():\n    \"\"\"Return a list of compiled regex patterns from existing step files.\"\"\"\n    patterns = []\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"steps_*.go\")):\n        if \"stub_\" in path.name:\n            continue\n        try:\n            content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        for m in EXISTING_REGEX_RE.finditer(content):\n            raw = m.group(1)\n            try:\n                patterns.append(re.compile(raw))\n            except re.error:\n                pass\n    return patterns\n\n\ndef matched_by_existing(text: str, patterns: list) -> bool:\n    for p in patterns:\n        if p.search(text):\n            return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef main():\n    locs = read_locations(OUT_TXT)\n    if not locs:\n        print(f\"no locations found in {OUT_TXT}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return 1\n\n    existing = load_existing_regex()\n    print(f\"loaded {len(existing)} existing step regex patterns\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    skipped_existing = 0\n    missing_files = set()\n    for status, rel, lineno in locs:\n        text = step_text(ROOT / rel, lineno)\n        if text is None:\n            missing_files.add(rel)\n            continue\n        text = strip_step_keyword(text)\n        if not text:\n            continue\n        if matched_by_existing(text, existing):\n            # Already covered by an existing handler; do not add a stub.\n            skipped_existing += 1\n            continue\n        epic = epic_of(rel)\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n            \"statuses\": set(),\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        info[\"statuses\"].add(status)\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"{rel}:{lineno}\")\n\n    if missing_files:\n        print(f\"warning: {len(missing_files)} files not found\", file=sys.stderr)\n    print(f\"skipped {skipped_existing} occurrences already covered by existing regex\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        f\"// Stub-определения для BDD-шагов эпика {epic}, которые иначе помечены\",\n        \"// как undefined в out.txt и не имеют существующих реализаций.\",\n        \"// Каждый шаг имеет конкретный regex (НЕ catch-all) и возвращает nil,\",\n        \"// чтобы godog не помечал шаг как undefined. Реальная семантика\",\n        \"// должна быть наполнена итеративно по приоритету эпика.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Подключение: RegisterStub*Steps вызывается per-epic из runner.go,\",\n        \"// после доменных registerEpicSteps. Stub'ы НЕ дублируют существующие\",\n        \"// regex — генератор отфильтровал тексты, которые уже покрыты доменными\",\n        \"// шагами в steps_*.go.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        statuses = \",\".join(sorted(info[\"statuses\"])) or \"?\"\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: {statuses} (sample: {sample})\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",

real and stub step files).\n\nReports:\n- texts that match no regex (would still be \"undefined\")\n- texts that match multiple regex (would be \"ambiguous\")\n- regex patterns that are duplicated (multiple registrations of identical pattern)\n\"\"\"\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\nFEATURES_DIR = ROOT / \"features\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\nREGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_steps_in_features():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, file, lineno, text) for every step line in feature files.\"\"\"\n    seen_keywords = STEP_KEYWORDS\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_table = False\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    in_table = False\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    in_table = True\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                # Step lines start with one of the keywords.\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in seen_keywords:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat, i, text)\n\n\ndef collect_registered_patterns_per_epic():\n    \"\"\"Return {epic: [(regex_str, file, line)]}.\n\n    Heuristic: stub_NN_* file targets epic NN. Other steps_*.go files target\n    the epic in their filename or are common (steps_common.go).\n    Register also based on runner.go's switch to know which Register* are\n    called per epic.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Parse runner.go to find which functions are called for each epic.\n    runner = (SUITE_DIR / \"runner.go\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    epic_funcs: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    case_re = re.compile(\n        r\"case len\\(epicName\\) >= 2 && epicName\\[:2\\] == \\\"(\\d+)\\\":\\s*(.*?)\\s*case \",\n        re.S,\n    )\n    # Append a sentinel to capture the last case.\n    runner_norm = runner + \"\\ncase XX\"\n    for m in case_re.finditer(runner_norm):\n        epic_num = m.group(1)\n        body = m.group(2)\n        for fm in re.finditer(r\"\\b(Register\\w+)\\(\", body):\n            epic_funcs[epic_num].append(fm.group(1))\n\n    # Map function name -> list of regex it registers.\n    func_regex: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"*.go\")):\n        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        # Find functions and parse out ctx.Step regex inside their body.\n        # Simplistic: split by \"func \".\n        # Use a state machine to find function boundaries.\n        i = 0\n        while True:\n            idx = text.find(\"\\nfunc \", i)\n            if idx == -1:\n                break\n            # function name\n            j = idx + len(\"\\nfunc \")\n            # Possibly receiver: func (x *T) Name(... or func Name(...\n            if text[j] == \"(\":\n                # skip receiver\n                close_recv = text.find(\")\", j)\n                if close_recv == -1:\n                    break\n                j = close_recv + 1\n                while j < len(text) and text[j] == \" \":\n                    j += 1\n            name_match = re.match(r\"(\\w+)\", text[j:])\n            if not name_match:\n                i = idx + 1\n                continue\n            fname = name_match.group(1)\n            body_start = text.find(\"{\", j)\n            if body_start == -1:\n                break\n            # find matching closing brace\n            depth = 0\n            k = body_start\n            while k < len(text):\n                c = text[k]\n                if c == \"{\":\n                    depth += 1\n                elif c == \"}\":\n                    depth -= 1\n                    if depth == 0:\n                        break\n                k += 1\n            body = text[body_start:k]\n            # capture all ctx.Step regex\n            for sm in REGEX_RE.finditer(body):\n                line_no = text.count(\"\\n\", 0, body_start + sm.start()) + 1\n                func_regex[fname].append((sm.group(1), path, line_no))\n            i = k\n\n    # Build epic -> list of registered patterns.\n    epic_patterns: dict[str, list[tuple[str, Path, int]]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for epic, funcs in epic_funcs.items():\n        for f in funcs:\n            for pat, path, line_no in func_regex.get(f, []):\n                epic_patterns[epic].append((pat, path, line_no))\n\n    return epic_patterns\n\n\ndef main():\n    epic_patterns = collect_registered_patterns_per_epic()\n    # Pre-compile patterns for matching.\n    epic_compiled: dict[str, list[tuple[re.Pattern, str, Path, int]]] = {}\n    for epic, items in epic_patterns.items():\n        compiled = []\n        for pat, path, line_no in items:\n            try:\n                compiled.append((re.compile(pat), pat, path, line_no))\n            except re.error as e:\n                print(f\"!!! invalid regex in {path}:{line_no}: {pat!r}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        epic_compiled[epic] = compiled\n\n    # Walk all feature step lines and check matches per epic.\n    undefined = defaultdict(list)\n    ambiguous = defaultdict(list)\n    matched = 0\n    for epic, feat, lineno, text in collect_steps_in_features():\n        epic_num = epic[:2]\n        compiled = epic_compiled.get(epic_num, [])\n        matches = [c for c in compiled if c[0].search(text)]\n        if not matches:\n            undefined[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text))\n        elif len(matches) > 1:\n            # Only consider it ambiguous if patterns differ\n            distinct = {m[1] for m in matches}\n            if len(distinct) > 1:\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n            else:\n                # Same regex registered multiple times - duplicate\n                ambiguous[epic].append((feat.name, lineno, text, distinct))\n        else:\n            matched += 1\n\n    total = matched + sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values()) + sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())\n    print(f\"Total feature step lines: {total}\")\n    print(f\"Matched (single):         {matched}\")\n    print(f\"Undefined:                {sum(len(v) for v in undefined.values())}\")\n    print(f\"Ambiguous/duplicate:      {sum(len(v) for v in ambiguous.values())}\")\n\n    if undefined:\n        print(\"\\n=== Undefined steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(undefined):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(undefined[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text in undefined[epic][:10]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n            if len(undefined[epic]) > 10:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(undefined[epic]) - 10} more\")\n\n    if ambiguous:\n        print(\"\\n=== Ambiguous steps per epic ===\")\n        for epic in sorted(ambiguous):\n            print(f\"\\n{epic} ({len(ambiguous[epic])}):\")\n            for fname, lineno, text, pats in ambiguous[epic][:5]:\n                print(f\"  {fname}:{lineno}  {text}\")\n                for p in list(pats)[:3]:\n                    print(f\"    matched by: {p}\")\n            if len(ambiguous[epic]) > 5:\n                print(f\"  ... and {len(ambiguous[epic]) - 5} more\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n",

r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\nEXISTING_REGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_feature_steps():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, feature_name, line_no, text) for every step line.\"\"\"\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat.name, i, text)\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef parse_funcs_in_file(path: Path):\n    \"\"\"Yield (func_name, regex_str) for ctx.Step calls within each function.\"\"\"\n    text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    i = 0\n    while True:\n        idx = text.find(\"\\nfunc \", i)\n        if idx == -1:\n            return\n        j = idx + len(\"\\nfunc \")\n        if j < len(text) and text[j] == \"(\":\n            close_recv = text.find(\")\", j)\n            if close_recv == -1:\n                return\n            j = close_recv + 1\n            while j < len(text) and text[j] == \" \":\n                j += 1\n        name_match = re.match(r\"(\\w+)\", text[j:])\n        if not name_match:\n            i = idx + 1\n            continue\n        fname = name_match.group(1)\n        body_start = text.find(\"{\", j)\n        if body_start == -1:\n            return\n        depth = 0\n        k = body_start\n        while k < len(text):\n            c = text[k]\n            if c == \"{\":\n                depth += 1\n            elif c == \"}\":\n                depth -= 1\n                if depth == 0:\n                    break\n            k += 1\n        body = text[body_start:k]\n        for sm in EXISTING_REGEX_RE.finditer(body):\n            yield (fname, sm.group(1))\n        i = k\n\n\ndef parse_runner_epic_funcs():\n    \"\"\"Return {epic_num: [Register* function names]} from runner.go switch.\"\"\"\n    runner = (SUITE_DIR / \"runner.go\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    epic_funcs: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    case_re = re.compile(\n        r\"case len\\(epicName\\) >= 2 && epicName\\[:2\\] == \\\"(\\d+)\\\":(.*?)(?=\\n\\tcase |\\n\\tdefault:)\",\n        re.S,\n    )\n    for m in case_re.finditer(runner):\n        epic_num = m.group(1)\n        body = m.group(2)\n        for fm in re.finditer(r\"\\b(Register\\w+)\\(\", body):\n            epic_funcs[epic_num].append(fm.group(1))\n    return epic_funcs\n\n\ndef build_per_epic_existing(skip_stubs: bool = True):\n    \"\"\"Return {epic_num: [compiled regex,...]}.\"\"\"\n    func_to_regex: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"steps_*.go\")):\n        if skip_stubs and \"stub_\" in path.name:\n            continue\n        for fname, regex in parse_funcs_in_file(path):\n            func_to_regex[fname].append(regex)\n\n    epic_funcs = parse_runner_epic_funcs()\n    epic_patterns: dict[str, list] = {}\n    for epic_num, funcs in epic_funcs.items():\n        compiled = []\n        for f in funcs:\n            for r in func_to_regex.get(f, []):\n                try:\n                    compiled.append((re.compile(r), r))\n                except re.error:\n                    pass\n        epic_patterns[epic_num] = compiled\n    return epic_patterns\n\n\ndef matched_by(text: str, compiled_pairs: list) -> bool:\n    for p, _ in compiled_pairs:\n        if p.search(text):\n            return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef main():\n    epic_patterns = build_per_epic_existing(skip_stubs=True)\n    counts = {e: len(p) for e, p in epic_patterns.items()}\n    print(f\"loaded existing per-epic patterns: {counts}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    skipped = 0\n    for epic, fname, lineno, text in collect_feature_steps():\n        epic_num = epic[:2]\n        existing = epic_patterns.get(epic_num, [])\n        if matched_by(text, existing):\n            skipped += 1\n            continue\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"features/{epic}/{fname}:{lineno}\")\n\n    print(f\"skipped {skipped} step lines already covered by existing per-epic regex\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        f\"// Stub-определения для BDD-шагов эпика {epic}, ещё не покрытых\",\n        \"// доменными реализациями. Каждый stub имеет конкретный regex (НЕ\",\n        \"// catch-all) и возвращает nil, чтобы godog не помечал шаг как\",\n        \"// undefined или pending. Реальная семантика наполняется итеративно\",\n        \"// по приоритету эпика — см. docs/BDD_STUB_NOTES.md.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Stub'ы регистрируются в registerEpicSteps (runner.go) ПОСЛЕ доменных\",\n        \"// шагов, поэтому при совпадении regex доменная реализация имеет приоритет.\",\n        \"// Генератор отфильтровал тексты, которые уже покрыты доменными regex,\",\n        \"// чтобы избежать дубликатов.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: sample {sample}\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n"

an existing handler regex.\n\nUses out.txt only as a hint to detect previously-pending steps; ultimately\nwe scan all .feature files to ensure every step has a handler so godog\nreports neither \"undefined\" nor \"pending\" after a fresh run.\n\nEach generated step uses a specific regex (no catch-all) and returns nil.\nReal semantics can be filled in later, per epic.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent\nSUITE_DIR = ROOT / \"features\" / \"suite\"\nFEATURES_DIR = ROOT / \"features\"\n\nSTEP_KEYWORDS = (\"Given\", \"When\", \"Then\", \"And\", \"But\", \"*\",\n                 \"Дано\", \"Когда\", \"Тогда\", \"И\", \"А\")\n\nRE_SPECIAL = r'.^$*+?()[]{}|\\\\'\n\nEXISTING_REGEX_RE = re.compile(r\"ctx\\.Step\\(\\s*`([^`]*)`\")\n\n\ndef strip_step_keyword(text: str) -> str:\n    s = text.lstrip()\n    for kw in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n        if s.startswith(kw + \" \"):\n            return s[len(kw) + 1:]\n        if s == kw:\n            return \"\"\n    return s\n\n\ndef collect_feature_steps():\n    \"\"\"Yield (epic, feature_name, line_no, text) for every step line.\"\"\"\n    for feature_dir in sorted(FEATURES_DIR.glob(\"[0-9][0-9]_*\")):\n        epic = feature_dir.name\n        for feat in sorted(feature_dir.glob(\"*.feature\")):\n            in_doc = False\n            for i, line in enumerate(feat.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").splitlines(), 1):\n                stripped = line.strip()\n                if not stripped:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith('\"\"\"'):\n                    in_doc = not in_doc\n                    continue\n                if in_doc:\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"|\"):\n                    continue\n                if stripped.startswith(\"#\") or stripped.startswith(\"@\"):\n                    continue\n                first = stripped.split(maxsplit=1)\n                if not first:\n                    continue\n                if first[0] in STEP_KEYWORDS:\n                    text = strip_step_keyword(stripped)\n                    if not text:\n                        continue\n                    yield (epic, feat.name, i, text)\n\n\ndef make_regex(text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert step text into a Go regex string anchored with ^...$.\n\n    Quoted args become \"([^\"]*)\"; <param> becomes <([^>]+)>; remaining regex\n    metacharacters are escaped. Cyrillic is kept verbatim.\n    \"\"\"\n    out = []\n    i = 0\n    n = len(text)\n    while i < n:\n        ch = text[i]\n        if ch == '\"':\n            j = text.find('\"', i + 1)\n            if j == -1:\n                out.append(re.escape(ch))\n                i += 1\n                continue\n            out.append(r'\"([^\"]*)\"')\n            i = j + 1\n            continue\n        if ch == '<':\n            j = text.find('>', i + 1)\n            if j != -1:\n                out.append(r'<([^>]+)>')\n                i = j + 1\n                continue\n        if ch in RE_SPECIAL:\n            out.append('\\\\' + ch)\n        else:\n            out.append(ch)\n        i += 1\n    return \"^\" + \"\".join(out) + \"$\"\n\n\ndef go_string_literal(s: str) -> str:\n    if \"`\" not in s:\n        return f\"`{s}`\"\n    escaped = s.replace(\"\\\\\", \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', '\\\\\"')\n    return f'\"{escaped}\"'\n\n\ndef step_arg_count(regex: str) -> int:\n    return regex.count('([^\"]*)') + regex.count('([^>]+)')\n\n\ndef is_data_table_step(text: str) -> bool:\n    return text.rstrip().endswith(\":\")\n\n\ndef load_existing_patterns():\n    \"\"\"Compile every regex registered by non-stub step files.\"\"\"\n    patterns = []\n    for path in sorted(SUITE_DIR.glob(\"steps_*.go\")):\n        if \"stub_\" in path.name:\n            continue\n        try:\n            content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        for m in EXISTING_REGEX_RE.finditer(content):\n            raw = m.group(1)\n            try:\n                patterns.append(re.compile(raw))\n            except re.error:\n                pass\n    return patterns\n\n\ndef matched_by(text: str, patterns: list) -> bool:\n    for p in patterns:\n        if p.search(text):\n            return True\n    return False\n\n\ndef main():\n    existing = load_existing_patterns()\n    print(f\"loaded {len(existing)} existing step regex patterns\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    epic_steps: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)\n    skipped = 0\n    for epic, fname, lineno, text in collect_feature_steps():\n        if matched_by(text, existing):\n            skipped += 1\n            continue\n        info = epic_steps[epic].setdefault(text, {\n            \"count\": 0,\n            \"samples\": [],\n        })\n        info[\"count\"] += 1\n        if len(info[\"samples\"]) < 1:\n            info[\"samples\"].append(f\"features/{epic}/{fname}:{lineno}\")\n\n    print(f\"skipped {skipped} step lines already covered by existing regex\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    summary = []\n    for epic in sorted(epic_steps):\n        steps = epic_steps[epic]\n        total_occurrences = sum(s[\"count\"] for s in steps.values())\n        summary.append((epic, len(steps), total_occurrences))\n        write_stub_file(epic, steps)\n\n    print(\"Generated stub files:\")\n    for epic, uniq, total in summary:\n        print(f\"  {epic}: {uniq} unique steps ({total} occurrences)\")\n    return 0\n\n\ndef write_stub_file(epic: str, steps: dict):\n    epic_prefix = epic.split(\"_\")[0]\n    suffix = \"_\".join(epic.split(\"_\")[1:]) or epic\n    fname = f\"steps_stub_{epic}.go\"\n    out_path = SUITE_DIR / fname\n\n    func_name = f\"RegisterStub{epic_prefix}{suffix.title().replace('_', '')}Steps\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"//go:build bdd\",\n        \"\",\n        \"// Code generated by experiments/gen_stubs.py. DO NOT EDIT.\",\n        \"//\",\n        f\"// Stub-определения для BDD-шагов эпика {epic}, ещё не покрытых\",\n        \"// доменными реализациями. Каждый stub имеет конкретный regex (НЕ\",\n        \"// catch-all) и возвращает nil, чтобы godog не помечал шаг как\",\n        \"// undefined или pending. Реальная семантика наполняется итеративно\",\n        \"// по приоритету эпика — см. docs/BDD_STUB_NOTES.md.\",\n        \"//\",\n        \"// Stub'ы регистрируются в registerEpicSteps (runner.go) ПОСЛЕ доменных\",\n        \"// шагов, поэтому при совпадении regex доменная реализация имеет приоритет.\",\n        \"// Генератор отфильтровал тексты, которые уже покрыты доменными regex,\",\n        \"// чтобы избежать дубликатов.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"package suite\",\n        \"\",\n        'import \"github.com/cucumber/godog\"',\n        \"\",\n        f\"// {func_name} регистрирует stub-шаги для эпика {epic}.\",\n        f\"func {func_name}(ctx *godog.ScenarioContext) {{\",\n    ]\n\n    sorted_texts = sorted(steps.keys())\n\n    seen_regex: set[str] = set()\n    for text in sorted_texts:\n        regex = make_regex(text)\n        if regex in seen_regex:\n            continue\n        seen_regex.add(regex)\n        info = steps[text]\n        arg_count = step_arg_count(regex)\n        has_table = is_data_table_step(text)\n        sample = info.get(\"samples\", [\"?\"])[0]\n        params = []\n        for _ in range(arg_count):\n            params.append(\"_ string\")\n        if has_table:\n            params.append(\"_ *godog.Table\")\n        sig = \", \".join(params)\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\t// stub: sample {sample}\")\n        lines.append(f\"\\tctx.Step({go_string_literal(regex)}, func({sig}) error {{ return nil }})\")\n\n    lines.append(\"}\")\n    lines.append(\"\")\n    out_path.write_text(\"\\n\".join(lines), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    sys.exit(main())\n",
